root_text.an3.54:1.1: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇaparibbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇaparibbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.54:1.2: “‘sandiṭṭhiko dhammo sandiṭṭhiko dhammo’ti, bho gotama, vuccati.
root_text.an3.54:1.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bho gotama, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī”ti?
root_text.an3.54:2.1: “Ratto kho, brāhmaṇa, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attabyābādhāyapi ceteti, parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.54:2.2: Rāge pahīne nevattabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.54:3.1: Ratto kho, brāhmaṇa, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.54:3.2: Rāge pahīne neva kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, na vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, na manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.54:4.1: Ratto kho, brāhmaṇa, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an3.54:4.2: Rāge pahīne attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.54:4.3: Evampi kho, brāhmaṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti …pe….
root_text.an3.54:5.2: mūḷho kho, brāhmaṇa, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attabyābādhāyapi ceteti, parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.54:5.3: Mohe pahīne nevattabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.54:6.1: Mūḷho kho, brāhmaṇa, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto, kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.54:6.2: Mohe pahīne neva kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, na vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, na manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.54:7.1: Mūḷho kho, brāhmaṇa, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an3.54:7.2: Mohe pahīne attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.54:7.3: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, sandiṭṭhiko dhammo hoti akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī”ti.
root_text.an3.54:8.2: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an3.54:1.1: Then a brahmin wanderer went up to the Buddha … Seated to one side he said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.54:1.2: “Worthy Gotama, they speak of ‘a teaching apparent in the present life’.
translation_text.an3.54:1.3: In what way is the teaching apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves?”
translation_text.an3.54:2.1: “A greedy person, overcome by greed, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.54:2.2: When greed has been given up, they don’t intend to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They don’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.54:3.1: A greedy person does bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.54:3.2: When greed has been given up, they don’t do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.54:4.1: A greedy person doesn’t truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.54:4.2: When greed has been given up, they truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.54:4.3: This is how the teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.an3.54:5.2: A deluded person, overcome by delusion, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.54:5.3: When delusion has been given up, they don’t intend to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They don’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.54:6.1: A deluded person does bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.54:6.2: When delusion has been given up, they don’t do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.54:7.1: A deluded person doesn’t truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.54:7.2: When delusion has been given up, they truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.54:7.3: This, too, is how the teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.”
translation_text.an3.54:8.2: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
